toolDefinitions.name: write_html
toolDefinitions.shortDescription: Write HTML with inline styles into the paywall. Converts to native Superwall nodes. Supports standard elements (div, p, img, svg, video, input) and custom sw-* elements (sw-lottie, sw-drawer, sw-picker, sw-slider, sw-navigation, sw-multiple-choice, sw-indicator). Use display:flex layout, padding/gap for spacing, and supported length units such as px, %, vw, vh, and dvh. Remote URLs auto-ingested. Modes: insert-children (append) or replace (clear + insert).
toolDefinitions.description:
Write HTML into the paywall. The HTML is parsed and converted into Superwall nodes. Supports standard HTML elements with inline CSS styles: -
,
,
, etc. -> stack (container) node -

,

-

, , text -> text node - -> img node (remote URLs are auto-ingested into first-party assets) - -> input node -

mode.description: insert-children adds as children, replace removes existing children first

toolDefinitions.name: begin_asset_upload
toolDefinitions.description: Start a direct upload session for a local image or video file. Returns a presigned upload URL and final public asset URL. Upload the local file directly to the returned uploadUrl, then call finish_asset_upload.

kind.description: Whether the upload is for an image or a video

mimeType.description: MIME type of the file, e.g. image/png or video/mp4

sizeBytes.description: Total file size in bytes, if known

toolDefinitions.name: finish_asset_upload
toolDefinitions.description: Finalize a direct upload after the file has been uploaded to the returned uploadUrl. Creates the asset record and returns the final asset URL and metadata.

uploadId.description: Upload session ID returned by begin_asset_upload

width.description: Optional width metadata for the uploaded asset, if known

height.description: Optional height metadata for the uploaded asset, if known

toolDefinitions.name: cancel_asset_upload
toolDefinitions.description: Cancel an in-progress direct upload session before finish_asset_upload is called.

uploadId.description: Upload session ID returned by begin_asset_upload

toolDefinitions.name: get_style_tokens
toolDefinitions.description: Get the current interface style tokens, available modes, and token values. Use this before theming or before referencing tokens from write_html via CSS like color: var(--sw-interface-text).

group.description: Optional style token group. Only "interface" is supported right now.

toolDefinitions.name: get_style_token_usage
toolDefinitions.description: Get which existing nodes and properties are currently bound to interface style tokens. Use this before updating or deleting a token to understand its blast radius.

group.description: Optional style token group. Only "interface" is supported right now.

names.description: Optional token names to inspect, e.g. ["text", "primary"]. Omit to inspect all interface tokens.

toolDefinitions.name: ensure_style_modes
toolDefinitions.description: Ensure the interface style token group has the requested modes. Use ["light"] for universal-only themes and ["light","dark"] to enable dark mode.

group.description: The style token group. Only "interface" is supported right now.

toolDefinitions.name: delete_style_modes
toolDefinitions.description: Delete removable interface style modes explicitly. This is destructive and only supports deleting non-primary modes like "dark".

group.description: The style token group. Only "interface" is supported right now.

modes.description: Modes to delete. For interface tokens, "light" cannot be deleted.

toolDefinitions.name: create_style_tokens
toolDefinitions.description: Create new interface style tokens. Tokens must not already exist. If a dark value is provided, dark mode must already exist.

group.description: The style token group. Only "interface" is supported right now.

toolDefinitions.name: update_style_tokens
toolDefinitions.description: Update existing interface style token values by mode. Tokens must already exist. If a dark value is provided, dark mode must already exist.

group.description: The style token group. Only "interface" is supported right now.

toolDefinitions.name: delete_style_tokens
toolDefinitions.description: Delete existing interface style tokens. This removes the token and all of its mode values. Tokens must already exist. Deletion fails if a token is still bound to nodes unless force is true.

group.description: The style token group. Only "interface" is supported right now.

force.description: When true, delete the token even if it is still bound to nodes. Prefer checking get_style_token_usage first.

toolDefinitions.name: bind_style_tokens
toolDefinitions.description: Bind existing node color styles to interface style tokens. Use CSS-style property names like color, backgroundColor, or borderColor and token refs like "interface.text".

styles.description: Map of CSS-style property names to token refs, e.g. {"color":"interface.text","backgroundColor":"interface.primary"}

toolDefinitions.name: get_products
toolDefinitions.description: Get the current paywall products together with the available application products that can be added. Use this before creating, deleting, or wiring product-specific UI.

toolDefinitions.name: create_product
toolDefinitions.description: Add a product to the current paywall from the available application products. This creates the paywall_product record and loads the derived state:products.<referenceName>.* values.

referenceName.description: Paywall reference name for this product, e.g. "primary", "yearly", or "trialPlan". It will be normalized to camelCase.

identifier.description: Existing application product identifier to add to the paywall.

toolDefinitions.name: delete_products
toolDefinitions.description: Delete one or more products from the current paywall by reference name. This removes both the paywall_product record and the derived state:products.<referenceName>.* data.

referenceNames.description: Paywall product reference names to delete, e.g. ["primary"] or ["yearly","monthly"].

toolDefinitions.name: get_localization_languages
toolDefinitions.description: Get the simple localization languages currently configured on the paywall.

toolDefinitions.name: add_localization_languages
toolDefinitions.description: Add simple localization languages to the paywall by creating paywall-language records for the given locale codes. This only configures the languages; it does not translate content or change preview locale.

toolDefinitions.name: delete_localization_languages
toolDefinitions.description: Delete simple localization languages from the paywall by removing the matching paywall-language records.

toolDefinitions.name: ai_localize
toolDefinitions.description: Trigger AI localization for the currently configured simple-localization languages and wait for it to finish. This fills missing translations for supported localizable content in the paywall.

toolDefinitions.name: get_variables
toolDefinitions.description: Get variables across the current paywall and runtime namespaces. This includes authored state.* variables, params.* parameters, products.*, device.*, user.*, permissions.*, callbacks.*, and element.* variables. Returns initial, current, and effective values for each variable.

group.description: Optional variable group to filter by.

names.description: Optional variable names within the selected group, e.g. ["isOpen"] or ["primary.identifier"].

ids.description: Optional exact variable IDs, e.g. ["state:state.isOpen"] or ["state:products.selectedIndex"].

toolDefinitions.name: get_variable_usage
toolDefinitions.description: Get where variables are currently used in node properties, conditionals, liquid rendering, or click behavior. Use this before deleting variables or when you need to understand interactive logic.

group.description: Optional variable group to filter by.

names.description: Optional variable names within the selected group.

ids.description: Optional exact variable IDs to inspect.

toolDefinitions.name: create_variables
toolDefinitions.description: Create authored paywall variables. Use kind='state' for paywall-local interaction state and kind='parameter' for values that may be supplied from the SDK when the paywall opens.

kind.description: Whether to create a paywall-local state or an SDK-backed parameter.

name.description: Variable name. States are normalized to camelCase. Parameters preserve the provided name so they can match SDK-provided values.

initialValue.description: Initial/default value when the paywall opens.

currentValue.description: Optional current in-session value to set immediately.

toolDefinitions.name: update_variable_initial_values
toolDefinitions.description: Update initial/default values for authored state.* or params.* variables. This changes the value new paywall sessions start with.

toolDefinitions.name: update_variable_current_values
toolDefinitions.description: Update current in-session values for mutable variables. Use this to preview interactions or put the paywall into a specific live state without changing defaults.

toolDefinitions.name: reset_variable_current_values
toolDefinitions.description: Clear current in-session overrides for variables so they fall back to their initial/default values again.

ids.description: Exact variable IDs whose current overrides should be cleared.

toolDefinitions.name: delete_variables
toolDefinitions.description: Delete authored state.* or params.* variables. Deletion fails if a variable is still in use unless force is true.

force.description: When true, delete variables even if they are still in use. Prefer checking get_variable_usage first.

toolDefinitions.name: set_dynamic_value
toolDefinitions.description: Set a dynamic/conditional value on an existing property. Cases are evaluated in order, first match wins. Use an empty query (rules: []) as the final fallback case. If no unconditional fallback case is provided, the existing value becomes the fallback automatically by default.

property.description: Friendly property name like backgroundColor, color, text, stack, input, slider, or clickBehavior.

cases.description: Ordered conditional cases. A case with query.rules=[] is the unconditional fallback and must be last.

query.description: React Query Builder style query object with a combinator and rules. Use rules: [] for the fallback case.

value.description: Branch value. For CSS properties use a native CSS value like '#fff' or '16px'. For text use a string. For other props use the literal property payload object.

preserveExistingFallback.description: Defaults to true. When no unconditional fallback case is provided, append the current value as the final fallback when possible.

toolDefinitions.name: clear_dynamic_value
toolDefinitions.description: Clear a dynamic/conditional value and collapse it back to its unconditional fallback case. This only works when the property has a final case with query.rules=[].

property.description: Friendly property name like backgroundColor, color, text, stack, input, slider, or clickBehavior.

toolDefinitions.name: set_click_behavior
toolDefinitions.description: Set tap/click behavior on existing nodes. Supports the full click-behavior action schema, including purchase flows, state updates, product selection, permissions, callbacks, scrolling, focus, delay, animation, haptics, controller buttons, and back-button behavior. Pass plain action objects; the tool handles action wrapper IDs. For navigation flows, use the action type "navigate-page" with direction "next" or "back" rather than a made-up "navigate" action.

actions.description: Ordered action list. Use plain typed action objects like { type: 'purchase', ... } or { type: 'set-state', stateId: 'state.toggled', operation: { type: 'toggle' } }. Nested request-permission and request-callback branches also take plain action objects.

preserveLockedActions.description: Defaults to true. Preserve any existing locked actions on the node and prepend them to the new behavior.

toolDefinitions.name: clear_click_behavior
toolDefinitions.description: Remove tap/click behavior from nodes. By default, locked actions are preserved if the node already has them.

preserveLockedActions.description: Defaults to true. Preserve locked actions if present instead of clearing them.

toolDefinitions.name: get_basic_info
toolDefinitions.description: Get overview of the current paywall: page ID, total node count, and root-level nodes.

toolDefinitions.name: get_subtree
toolDefinitions.description: Get a structured JSON subtree for a node or page. Useful for understanding hierarchy, sibling order, child counts, and where a node sits before editing or moving it.

nodeId.description: Root node or page ID (defaults to current page)

includeProperties.description: Whether to include css:* and prop:* properties on each returned node

toolDefinitions.name: get_selection
toolDefinitions.description: Get the currently selected nodes in the editor.

toolDefinitions.name: get_node_info
toolDefinitions.description: Get detailed information about a specific node: type, name, parent, children, and all CSS/prop properties.

toolDefinitions.name: get_children
toolDefinitions.description: Get the direct children of a node with their IDs, types, names, and child counts.

toolDefinitions.name: get_navigation_pages
toolDefinitions.description: Get the direct page children of a navigation node in flow order. Use this before wiring routes so you have the actual page node IDs.

toolDefinitions.name: get_navigation_flow
toolDefinitions.description: Get the transition, entry connection, and page-to-page connections for a navigation node. Use this to inspect the current navigation flow before editing it.

toolDefinitions.name: set_default_navigation_transition
toolDefinitions.description: Set the default transition on a navigation node. Valid values are "none", "push", "fade", "slide", and "fade_slide".

transition.description: Default transition for routes that do not override it.

toolDefinitions.name: set_navigation_entry_connection
toolDefinitions.description: Replace the navigation entry connection. Use this to define which page the flow starts on and to support conditional branching at entry.

branches.description: Ordered entry branches. The first matching query wins. Use an empty rules array for the default/fallback branch.

id.description: Optional stable branch ID. Omit to auto-generate.

targetPageId.description: Navigation child page node ID to enter.

transition.description: Optional transition override for this branch.

query.description: Optional query-builder rule group for conditional branching. Omit or use rules: [] for the fallback branch.

toolDefinitions.name: set_navigation_connection
toolDefinitions.description: Create or replace the connection from one navigation page to one or more target pages. Use this for forward flow branching and optional auto-transition timing.

sourcePageId.description: Navigation child page node ID the connection starts from.

autoTransitionDelayS.description: Optional delay in seconds for automatic transition after this page settles.

branches.description: Ordered connection branches. The first matching query wins. Use an empty rules array for the default/fallback branch.

id.description: Optional stable branch ID. Omit to auto-generate.

targetPageId.description: Navigation child page node ID to navigate to.

transition.description: Optional transition override for this branch.

query.description: Optional query-builder rule group for conditional branching. Omit or use rules: [] for the fallback branch.

toolDefinitions.name: delete_navigation_connection
toolDefinitions.description: Delete a page-to-page navigation connection by its source page. This does not delete the page itself.

sourcePageId.description: Source page node ID whose connection should be removed.

toolDefinitions.name: get_computed_styles
toolDefinitions.description: Get the computed CSS styles for one or more nodes. Returns a map of nodeId -> CSS properties.

toolDefinitions.name: get_screenshot
toolDefinitions.description: Capture a screenshot of a specific node by ID. Returns the image as base64-encoded data. Images are automatically capped to fit API size limits. Defaults to 1x scale which is sufficient for verifying layout, spacing, and visual appearance. Use scale=2 only when you need to read small text or inspect fine visual details. By default overlays device details (status bar, notch/dynamic island, home indicator) on top of the full scrollable content so you can see where OS chrome sits.

nodeId.description: The ID of the node to capture

scale.description: Render scale factor. 1 (default) for layout checks. 2 for reading small text.

transparent.description: Whether the image should have a transparent background. If true, returns PNG; otherwise JPEG.

showDeviceDetails.description: Whether to overlay device details (status bar, notch/dynamic island, home indicator) on the screenshot. Defaults to true. These are drawn on top of the full scrollable content — nothing is clipped. Set to false for a clean content-only screenshot.

clipToViewport.description: Whether to crop the screenshot to the device viewport height, showing only what the user sees without scrolling. Defaults to false (captures full scrollable content). Useful for checking above-the-fold layout and CTA visibility.

toolDefinitions.name: update_styles
toolDefinitions.description: Update CSS styles on one or more nodes. Supports batch updates.

toolDefinitions.name: set_text_content
toolDefinitions.description: Set the text content of one or more text nodes. This overwrites any existing dynamic text binding with a static value.

toolDefinitions.name: rename_nodes
toolDefinitions.description: Rename one or more nodes in the layer tree.

toolDefinitions.name: duplicate_nodes
toolDefinitions.description: Duplicate one or more nodes. Creates a copy after each source node.

toolDefinitions.name: move_nodes
toolDefinitions.description: Move existing nodes relative to other nodes. Use this for reparenting or repositioning existing content instead of rewriting HTML. "inside" reparents the node as the last child of the target container.

targetNodeId.description: Reference node to move relative to

position.description: before/after keep the same parent as the target; inside reparents into the target

toolDefinitions.name: reorder_children
toolDefinitions.description: Reorder direct children of a parent by supplying child IDs in the desired order. This is useful when the right nodes already exist and only their sibling order should change.

orderedChildIds.description: Direct child IDs in the desired order

preserveUnspecified.description: If true (default), children not listed stay after the specified ones in their current order

toolDefinitions.name: delete_nodes
toolDefinitions.description: Delete one or more nodes and all their descendants.

toolDefinitions.name: get_notification
toolDefinitions.description: Get the current trial-started notification configuration. Returns text fields (title, subtitle, body), timing, and whether each field is dynamic/conditional.

toolDefinitions.name: set_notification
toolDefinitions.description: Create or update the trial-started notification. All fields are optional — only provided fields are updated. Timing accepts human-readable durations like "3 days", "12 hours", "1 day 6 hours". If creating a new notification, omitted timing defaults to 3 days. Text updates preserve existing localizations.

timing.description: How long before trial end to send the notification. Human-readable duration like "3 days", "12 hours", "1 day 6 hours", or milliseconds as a number string.

toolDefinitions.name: delete_notification
toolDefinitions.description: Remove the trial-started notification entirely.

toolDefinitions.name: set_notification_dynamic_value
toolDefinitions.description: Set a dynamic/conditional value on a notification field. Cases are evaluated in order, first match wins. Use an empty query (rules: []) as the final fallback case. Same query syntax as set_dynamic_value.

field.description: The notification field to make dynamic.

cases.description: Ordered conditional cases. A case with query.rules=[] is the unconditional fallback and must be last.

query.description: React Query Builder style query with combinator and rules. Use rules: [] for fallback.

value.description: For text fields (title/subtitle/body): a string. For before_trial_end: a duration string like "3 days" or milliseconds number.

toolDefinitions.name: clear_notification_dynamic_value
toolDefinitions.description: Clear a dynamic/conditional value on a notification field, collapsing it back to its unconditional fallback.

toolDefinitions.name: start_working_on_nodes
toolDefinitions.description: Mark nodes as being actively worked on. Shows a working indicator on those nodes and updates the editor status. Call this before starting modifications to a section of the paywall.

nodeIds.description: Node IDs to mark as being worked on. Use 'page:page' to mark the entire paywall.

toolDefinitions.name: finish_working_on_nodes
toolDefinitions.description: Clear the working indicator from nodes. Call this after completing modifications. If nodeIds is omitted, clears all working indicators.

nodeIds.description: Node IDs to clear. Omit to clear all.

toolDefinitions.name: search_nodes
toolDefinitions.description: Search for nodes matching a regex pattern across names, stored properties, text content, and input placeholders.

page.description: Page number (10 results per page, default 0)

toolDefinitions.name: find_replace
toolDefinitions.description: Find and replace values across all nodes. Useful for bulk color, text, or URL changes.
